posts.title: All Yono Games: Complete List of Yono Android Apps
posts.excerpt: A complete directory of every Yono-network Android game app, organised by category — 59 apps covering Rummy, Teen Patti, Spin, Slots, and Arcade formats.
posts.author: AllYonoGuru Team
posts.content:
AllYonoGuru tracks every Android app in the Yono network. This page lists all 59 apps currently in our directory, sorted by game type. Each entry links to the full app page where you can read a detailed description, find the download link, and check device requirements.

The apps below span four categories: Rummy card games, Teen Patti and mixed card games, spin-format games, and slots titles.

## Rummy Games (19 apps)

These apps focus on the 13-card Indian Rummy format. Most support Points, Pool, and Deals variants. A few specialise in particular formats or player types.

- [Rummy Guru](/rummy-guru) — Points, Pool, and Deals Rummy with an offline practice mode and in-app performance stats panel.
- [Jaiho Rummy](/jaiho-rummy) — Practice-first design with structured offline drills that focus on drop-or-continue decision making.
- [Yono Rummy](/yono-rummy) — Fast matchmaking across all three table formats with queue times typically under a few seconds.
- [ABC Rummy](/abc-rummy) — Beginner-friendly with inline tooltips, colour-coded meld badges, and a persistent rule reference on every screen.
- [Boss Rummy](/boss-rummy) — Daily elimination bracket that resets at midnight, with bracket history and live standings.
- [Game Rummy](/game-rummy) — Frame-perfect deal animation and haptic feedback on every draw and discard interaction.
- [Gogo Rummy](/gogo-rummy) — Tracks your record separately by variant and shows estimated session lengths in the lobby for each format.
- [Hi Rummy](/hi-rummy) — Under 20 MB install, optimised for older Android phones with limited storage and RAM.
- [Ind Rummy](/ind-rummy) — Regional Indian Rummy rules including household conventions on joker handling and drop scoring.
- [INR Rummy](/inr-rummy) — Switch between Points, Pool, and Deals mid-session without returning to the lobby.
- [Joy Rummy](/joy-rummy) — Social design with avatar expressions, ambient sounds, and experience-matched newcomer matchmaking.
- [Love Rummy](/love-rummy) — Loose-flick discard gesture and a thumb-reachable layout for accurate one-handed play.
- [OkRummy](/ok-rummy) — Measures pure sequence formation speed, drop accuracy, and discard quality with individual skill progression lines.
- [Rumble Rummy](/rumble-rummy) — Head-to-head elimination tables with per-opponent discard history for future match reference.
- [Rummy 77](/rummy-77) — 77-card extended shoe format where the deeper deck rewards discard memory across longer hands.
- [Rummy 91](/rummy-91) — Specialist 91-points Pool variant with live elimination margins visible for every seated player.
- [Rummy 888](/rummy-888) — GPU-accelerated rendering tuned for smooth frame timing during deal sequences and meld animations.
- [Rummy Ludo](/rummy-ludo) — Bundles 13-card Rummy and four-token Ludo with configurable house rules in one install.
- [Top Rummy](/top-rummy) — Advanced analytics tracking discard-regret patterns and declaration timing, aimed at experienced players.

## Teen Patti and Card Game Apps (16 apps)

Teen Patti is a three-card betting game and India's most played card format. The apps in this section cover Teen Patti variants, hybrid card-and-board combinations, prediction formats, and multi-game launchers.

- [Teen Patti Guru](/teen-patti-guru) — Classic, AK47, Muflis, and Joker variants with offline practice and live table modes.
- [Jaiho Arcade](/jaiho-arcade) — Short-session mini-game launcher covering dice, card, and prediction formats under one install.
- [Jaiho Arcade+](/jaiho-arcade-plus) — Expanded version with persistent multiplayer rooms and richer variant mechanics.
- [Jaiho 91](/jaiho-91) — Three-card game with a 91-style extended stake structure and opponent bet-progression tracking.
- [Jaiho Win](/jaiho-win) — Combines three-card and Rummy tables with Snakes and Ladders and a token-chase board game.
- [Yono Arcade](/yono-arcade) — Fast-launch multi-game app that opens directly into your last played title with minimal cold-start time.
- [Yono Games](/yono-games) — Multi-game platform covering cards, board games, and prediction formats with a shared friends list.
- [Yono VIP](/yono-vip) — Premium Yono tier with curated game formats, dedicated table moderation, and capped lobby capacity.
- [Bet 213](/bet-213) — Prediction format built around a 2-1-3 round-phase structure for consistent pacing.
- [Bingo 101](/bingo-101) — Indian Bingo with card selection and mid-round pattern switching for more active decision making.
- [Club INR](/club-inr) — Club-style lobby with a shared member roster visible across three-card, Rummy, and board game tables.
- [Ind Bingo](/ind-bingo) — Regional Indian Bingo variant with Hindi calling by default and household-style card-grid spacing.
- [Ind Club](/ind-club) — Traditional Indian card and board game formats preserved with regional rule fidelity.
- [Maha Games](/maha-games) — Wide catalogue of Indian skill formats with category and region filters for discovery.
- [MBM Bet](/mbm-bet) — Prediction format with planning phases and deliberate pacing for strategic decision making.
- [Neta VIP](/neta-vip) — Tiered lobby system where members unlock progressively exclusive tables through consistent activity.

## Spin Format Apps (10 apps)

Spin games are fast-round pattern-recognition and prediction formats where reading round structure and recognising combinations is the core skill.

- [Jaiho Spin](/jaiho-spin) — Multiple spin mode variations under one home screen, each with different round pacing and decision depth.
- [Spin 101](/spin-101) — Entry-level spin format for newcomers with plain-language tutorials and guided visual feedback.
- [Spin 777](/spin-777) — Pattern recognition centred on the triple-seven combination with per-round response-time tracking.
- [Spin Crush](/spin-crush) — Adds mid-round intervention decisions and counterfactual post-round summaries for strategic depth.
- [Spin Gold](/spin-gold) — Custom illustrated assets and hand-tuned motion design for a polished visual experience.
- [Spin Lucky](/spin-lucky) — Lightweight design that runs on phones with 1.5 GB RAM with battery use kept low between rounds.
- [Spin Winner](/spin-winner) — Competitive spin tables with skill-rating matchmaking for consistently contested sessions.
- [Yes Spin](/yes-spin) — See the full description on the [Yes Spin](/yes-spin) app page.
- [Slot Spin](/slot-spin) — Hybrid spin and slot format. Full description on the [Slot Spin](/slot-spin) app page.
- [101z](/101z) — Spin format with extended round structure. Full description on the [101z](/101z) app page.

## Slots and Casino-Style Apps (14 apps)

These apps feature slot mechanics and arcade-format titles in the Yono network. Full descriptions and download links are on each individual app page.

- [567 Slots](/567-slots)
- [777 Game](/777-game)
- [789 Jackpot](/789-jackpot)
- [Hindi 777](/hindi777)
- [Ind Slots](/ind-slots)
- [Jaiho 777](/jaiho-777)
- [Jaiho Slot](/jaiho-slot)
- [Jahio 777](/jahio-777)
- [Saga Slots](/saga-slots)
- [Share Slots](/share-slots)
- [Slots Winner](/slots-winner)
- [Yn 777](/yn-777)
- [Yono 777](/yono-777)
- [Yono Slots](/yono-slots)

## About This Directory

AllYonoGuru is an independent listing site. We are not affiliated with the Yono network or any of the app developers listed here. All apps are listed for informational purposes. Age restrictions (18+) and state-level availability apply — see our [disclaimer](/disclaimer) for details.

If an app is missing or a detail has changed, reach us via our [contact page](/contact). To understand the Rummy format before downloading, our [Rummy sequence rules guide](/blog/rummy-sequence-rules-pure-sequence-explained) covers the essentials.

posts.title: All Rummy Games: The Complete Rummy App List for Android
posts.excerpt: Every Rummy app in the AllYonoGuru directory in one place — 19 apps covering Points, Pool, Deals, head-to-head, and specialty Rummy formats for Android.
posts.author: AllYonoGuru Team
posts.content:
AllYonoGuru lists 19 Rummy apps covering the full range of 13-card Indian Rummy formats. This page brings them all together so you can compare at a glance and find the one that fits how you like to play.

All apps are Android APKs available for direct download. Each name below links to the full app page with a detailed description, device requirements, and the download link.

## Featured Rummy App

[Rummy Guru](/rummy-guru) is the most reviewed app in our Rummy section and a solid default if you are deciding where to start. It covers Points, Pool, and Deals Rummy in one install, with an offline practice mode for drilling sequence recognition and a stats panel that tracks your performance across sessions.

## All Rummy Apps, A to Z

**[ABC Rummy](/abc-rummy)** is designed for new players. Inline tooltips explain every decision point, colour-coded badges on your hand flag whether your pure sequence condition is met or still open, and a searchable rule glossary stays accessible from any screen without backing out of your current table.

**[Boss Rummy](/boss-rummy)** runs a daily elimination ladder that resets at midnight. When you join, you enter a bracket that advances through rounds across the day. Your position on the live leaderboard updates after each hand, and bracket history is archived so you can review past elimination points.

**[Game Rummy](/game-rummy)** focuses on presentation. Each deal uses a smooth arc-and-settle motion and completed melds animate with a small lift. Haptic feedback confirms every draw and discard, giving the feel of handling physical cards rather than tapping a flat screen.

**[Gogo Rummy](/gogo-rummy)** tracks your record separately for Points, Pool, and Deals so you can see whether you perform better in quick single-hand rounds or longer accumulation-style sessions. The lobby labels each format with an estimated session length before you commit to a seat.

**[Hi Rummy](/hi-rummy)** installs at under 20 MB and is optimised to run on phones with 2 GB of RAM. If other card apps stutter or take a long time to load on your device, this is the right choice for your hardware.

**[Ind Rummy](/ind-rummy)** implements regional Indian Rummy rules rather than a single international standard. The app supports household-table conventions including variations in how printed jokers substitute inside sequences and how drop scoring is calculated. Each table lists its active rule set in the lobby before you sit down.

**[INR Rummy](/inr-rummy)** lets you switch between Points, Pool, and Deals mid-session from a variant picker at any table. Switching does not reload assets and your previous table is held in a paused state briefly so you can return without losing your seat.

**[Jaiho Rummy](/jaiho-rummy)** takes a practice-first approach. The offline drill library puts you in specific decision positions — when to take an early drop, when to push through a marginal hand, and when to declare — and the same scenario can be replayed until the correct read becomes automatic.

**[Joy Rummy](/joy-rummy)** adds a social layer to the standard Rummy table. Player avatars display expressions tied to table events, ambient sounds give a sense of a gathering rather than an empty digital felt, and lower-stakes tables match newcomers with players of comparable experience.

**[Love Rummy](/love-rummy)** addresses a common frustration in card apps: mis-discards under time pressure. The discard interface accepts a loose flick gesture in the general direction of the discard pile and uses the swipe vector to identify which card you intended, rather than requiring a precise tap on a small target.

**[OkRummy](/ok-rummy)** treats skill development as something measurable. The progression dashboard tracks pure sequence formation speed, drop decision accuracy, and average discard quality with separate lines, and milestones unlock at concrete performance thresholds rather than through time-in-app.

**[Rumble Rummy](/rumble-rummy)** strips the table to its most focused competitive form: one opponent across multiple hands in a head-to-head elimination bracket. Per-opponent discard history is preserved so if you face the same player again, you can review how they discarded under pressure last time.

**[Rummy 77](/rummy-77)** uses a 77-card extended shoe rather than the standard deck. The deeper pool means any specific card value stays live across more turns, which shifts discard reads significantly. The app's tutorial covers which standard reads carry over and which become misleading in the extended format.

**[Rummy 91](/rummy-91)** specialises in the 91-points Pool variant. The higher elimination threshold changes how costly an early drop is relative to the standard 101 format, and the lobby displays every player's remaining margin in real time so you can adjust your reads as the session progresses.

**[Rummy 888](/rummy-888)** puts engineering effort into frame-rate consistency. The rendering pipeline uses GPU-accelerated sprites to maintain steady frame timing during deal sequences and meld animations, which is where most card apps produce visible stutter on mid-range hardware.

**[Rummy Ludo](/rummy-ludo)** bundles two complete games: 13-card Rummy with standard format variants and four-token Ludo with configurable house rules for capture chains and safe squares. Switching between them is a single tap from the home screen, and only the active mode loads into memory at any time.

**[Top Rummy](/top-rummy)** is built for players who have outgrown introductory apps. The analytics layer tracks advanced patterns including your most common discard-then-regret sequences and the table positions where your declaration timing tends to slip. The app does not run a beginner mode by design.

**[Yono Rummy](/yono-rummy)** prioritises matchmaking speed. Points, all Pool lengths, and Deals format draw from a shared player pool simultaneously, and average queue time stays in the low single digits during peak hours. Switching between formats in the lobby does not require reloading table assets.

## Which App Should You Start With?

If you are new to Rummy on Android, [ABC Rummy](/abc-rummy) or [Joy Rummy](/joy-rummy) are the most welcoming starting points.

If you want to practice offline before live tables, [Jaiho Rummy](/jaiho-rummy) or [Rummy Guru](/rummy-guru) both have dedicated offline modes.

If you play on an older or budget Android phone, [Hi Rummy](/hi-rummy) is specifically built for that hardware range.

If you prefer competitive one-on-one play, [Rumble Rummy](/rumble-rummy) runs head-to-head elimination tables with sustained focus on a single opponent.

If you want all three main formats in one place, [Yono Rummy](/yono-rummy) or [Gogo Rummy](/gogo-rummy) both cover Points, Pool, and Deals with quick switching between formats.

---

All apps are independent APK downloads. Age restrictions (18+) and state-level availability apply — see our [disclaimer](/disclaimer). To understand the game rules before downloading, our [Rummy sequence rules guide](/blog/rummy-sequence-rules-pure-sequence-explained) explains pure sequences, sets, and valid hand combinations from the ground up.
